summary: My grandfather was born in Galway, Ireland. It was where his life began before he moved on to Dublin. Galway holds a special place in our family history as the starting point of my grandfather's journey.
Ireland is where my family roots run deep. My grandfather was born there, specifically in Galway, and later lived in Dublin. It's the country that shaped much of my family's early life and heritage.

My grandfather's father came from Scotland, specifically from Edinburgh. He was a Scottish man who moved to Ireland and became an Episcopalian minister in Dublin. Scotland, therefore, is part of my family's lineage and story.

Edinburgh is where my grandfather's father originated before moving to Ireland. He left Edinburgh to become an Episcopalian minister in Dublin, which was quite a change. This city marks the Scottish side of my family's background.

After being born in Galway, my grandfather lived in Dublin for a time. It was also where my Scottish great-grandfather served as an Episcopalian minister. Dublin represents an important chapter in my family's history in Ireland.

My father's life eventually brought him to San Francisco, which is where our family's story continued in the United States. Before that, my grandfather and great-grandfather had their lives in Ireland and Scotland. San Francisco marks the new chapter of our family's journey abroad.
